3 MQTT Control Packets -> 3.1 CONNECT Co.data:
{
    "MUST be used by Clients and by Servers to identify state": "The ClientID MUST be used by Clients and by Servers to identify state that the Clients and Servers hold relating to this MQTT Session between the Client and the Server [MQTT-3.1.3-2]",
    "MUST be present": "The ClientID MUST be present and is the first field in the CONNECT packet Payload [MQTT-3.1.3-3]",
    "MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String": "The ClientID MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String as defined in section 1.5.4 [MQTT-3.1.3-4]",
    "MUST allow ClientIDs which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length": "The Server MUST allow ClientIDs which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length, and that contain only the characters \"0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ\" [MQTT-3.1.3-5]",
    "MUST treat this as a special case": "however if the Server allows a zero-byte ClientID, the Server MUST treat this as a special case and assign a unique ClientID to that Client [MQTT-3.1.3-6]",
    "MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID": "The Server MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID, and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet [MQTT-3.1.3-7]",
    "MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet": "The Server MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID, and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet [MQTT-3.1.3-7]",
    "MUST close the Network Connection": "If the Server rejects the ClientID, the Server MAY respond to the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK using Reason Code 0x85 (Client Identifier not valid) as described in section 4.13 Handling errors, and then the Server MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-3.1.3-8]"
}

mandatory: the Server SHOULD close the Network Connection

The Server MUST perform the processing of Clean Start that is described in section 3.1.2.4
The Server MUST acknowledge the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK packet containing a 0x00 (Success) Reason Code
If the Server rejects the CONNECT, the Server MUST NOT process any data sent by the Client after the CONNECT packet except AUTH packets
recommended: the Server SHOULD close the Network Connection
SHOULD perform authentication and authorization checks

MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters
MUST NOT: MUST NOT include the null character
MUST NOT encode to more than 65,535 bytes
MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters

Recommended: It is strongly recommended that Server implementations that offer TLS should use TCP port 8883

Prohibited: should not be included

Mandatory: is allowed to never send
is allowed to ignore
Prohibited: cannot contain
Recommended: can be used to allow for

5 Security (non-normative) -> 5.4 Implem.data:
{
    "would normally choose to validate": "SHOULD",
    "avoids implementation difficulties": "SHOULD",
    "protects applications from having to process them": "SHOULD",
    "removes some security exposures": "SHOULD",
    "are not used": "SHOULD NOT",
    "are commonly used as special markers": "SHOULD NOT",
    "could permit such exploits": "SHOULD NOT"
}

mandatory: MUST close the Network Connection
will not send Malformed Packets or cause Protocol Errors
prohibited: will not send Malformed Packets or cause Protocol Errors
recommended: SHOULD send a DISCONNECT packet containing the reason code before closing the Network Connection

Mandatory: should be aware
should support
should be aware

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry
MUST NOT: MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case

}

Mandatory: MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames
MUST close the Network Connection
MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols the Client offers
MUST be mqtt

MUST: MUST respond with the packet as determined by the QoS in the PUBLISH Packet
MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions
MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions
MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have a Subscription Identifier
MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if it has a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT: MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
is a Protocol Error: is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in SUBSCRIBE packet which caused it to flow
A Topic Alias value of 0 or greater than the Maximum Topic Alias is a Protocol Error
if the receiver does not already have a mapping for this Topic Alias and the packet has a zero length Topic Name field it is a Protocol Error
uses a DISCONNECT packet: the Client uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x9B (QoS not supported)
the receiver uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code of 0x94 (Topic Alias invalid)
the receiver uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x82 (Protocol Error)
the Server uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)
the Client uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)
